source.body:
Summary

Purpose: This playbook defines a repeatable end-to-end flow from hiring decision to 90-day impact. It blends hiring committee practice, preboarding, a structured 30/60/90 plan, manager coaching checkpoints, and objective ramp metrics so new hires hit predictable outcomes. Use this as the canonical manager + people-ops coordination checklist for IC and manager hires.

Audience
- Hiring managers
- People Ops / Talent partners
- Interviewers and hiring committee members
- New hire onboarding sponsors and buddies

Key outcomes
- Time-to-autonomy target: role-dependent (see Ramp SLOs table)
- New-hire satisfaction (first 30 days) >= 85%
- Hiring decision consistency across committees via a shared rubric
- Manager-led coaching cadence established in first 14 days

1) Hiring and Decisioning: Structured Committee Workflow

Before finalizing an offer: 
1.1 Agenda for final hiring committee (owner: hiring manager, attendees: recruiter, 2+ interviewers, people-ops partner)
- Confirm role scope and top-3 success criteria (must be measurable)
- Review interview notes against rubric (skills, execution, communication, culture add, leveling evidence)
- Discuss candidate risks and mitigation plan (training, mentoring, pairing)
- Approve offer band and start date contingency

1.2 Decision rubric (use during debrief; required fields):
- Technical competence: Strong / Adequate / Weak
- Problem solving: Strong / Adequate / Weak
- Communication: Strong / Adequate / Weak
- Role fit & impact potential: High / Medium / Low
- Recommended next step: Hire / Hold / Reject

1.3 Offer timeline SLOs (people-ops owns SLA monitoring):
- Offer authorized within 48 hours of committee approval
- Candidate receives offer within 72 hours of authorization
- Offer sign/decline decision logged within 7 days

2) Preboarding (0–7 days before start)

Goals: reduce first-day friction, align expectations, and start technical access provisioning.

Preboarding checklist (owner: recruiter + people-ops):
- Send welcome packet with role-specific 30/60/90 preview and first-week agenda
- Collect equipment specs, desk/location preference, and KYC for access
- Initiate IT/infra ticket for hardware + software provisioning (target: ready by day 0)
- Assign buddy and onboarding sponsor; schedule intro calendar invites
- Share prework: team RFCs, architecture overviews (if applicable), and a list of 5 prioritized docs to read

Template subject line for first contact: "Welcome to Redwood — Your starter plan and orientation (start date: {date})"

3) Day 0–14: Orientation + Rapid Context Building

Manager responsibilities (first 14 days):
- Day 0 orientation: team mission, org chart, immediate expectations, access checklist review
- Schedule 1:1 cadence: 3x week quick sync for week 1, then weekly 1:1s thereafter
- First contribution: assign a "small win" task that touches production or a real team workflow
- Pairing: at least 2 pairing sessions with different teammates in week 1

Checklist for new hire first week (owner: manager + buddy):
- Complete security and compliance training
- Confirm dev environment build and CI access
- Run a smoke deployment / example workflow (or relevant domain equivalent)
- Submit first PR or customer-facing artifact (if role-appropriate)

4) 30/60/90 Activation Template (manager-owned)

Create a 30/60/90 plan document in the team wiki within 48 hours of start. Use this template fields:
- Impact objectives (3 maximum per window)
- Success criteria (quantitative where possible)
- Key stakeholders and dependencies
- Learning goals and resources
- Checkpoints & owner for each objective

Example (SRE engineer):
- 30d: Deliver a reproducible runbook for oncall escalation for service X (success: runbook tested in staging)
- 60d: Lead one postmortem with actionable follow-ups (success: 80% closure of action items within 30 days)
- 90d: Own a production rollout for a minor feature (success: <1% error regression)

5) Metrics & Ramp SLOs (People Ops + Eng SRE define targets)

Ramp targets (example bands)
| Role level | Time-to-autonomy (SLO) | Key ramp metric |
|---|---:|---|
| Jr IC (L2) | 45 days | First independent PR + buddy sign-off
| Mid IC (L3) | 60 days | First feature rollout + customer impact metric measured
| Senior (L4+) | 90 days | Lead cross-team initiative + measurable outcome

Manager should report progress at 30/60/90 checkpoints to people-ops for aggregate ramp analytics.

6) Coaching & Feedback Cadence

Structured manager playbook for feedback: 
- Week 1–2: focus on orientation, immediate blockers, and confidence-building (1:1 multiple times/week)
- Week 3–6: move to goal-setting discussions and tactical coaching (weekly 1:1 + biweekly skip-level sync suggestion)
- Week 7–12: performance calibration: provide measurable feedback, identify growth areas, and set up stretch projects

Deliverable: manager writes a short "30-day checkpoint memo" and shares with the hire and people-ops. Template: 3 bullet wins, 3 blockers, 2 next steps.

Calibration & mid-ramp corrections: if ramp metrics are off-track at 30 days, manager + people-ops will create a Performance Support Plan (PSP) with clear actions and 30-day recheck.

7) Performance Review Integration (first-cycle guidance)

- New hires enter standard performance cycle after 90 days unless role-specific probation applies.
- Managers map 30/60/90 goals to performance rubric when writing 90-day assessment.

8) Manager Tools & Templates (copyable artifacts)

Included templates (inlined in the wiki):
- 30/60/90 plan (fillable table)
- 30-day checkpoint memo (3 wins / 3 blockers / 2 next steps)
- Interviewer debrief form (rubric fields and evidence bullets)
- Preboarding email template and checklist
- PSP template with measurable milestones and owner

9) Escalations and People-ops Support

When to escalate to people-ops:
- Access or equipment not provisioned by day 3
- Onboarding blockers that prevent contribution beyond 7 days
- Behavioral or fit concerns observed in first 30 days
- If ramp SLO missed at 60 or behavioral risk identified

Escalation path: manager -> people-ops partner -> HR business partner -> (if required) legal or security. Document actions and dates in the hire's onboarding record.

10) Continuous Improvement & Measurement

Data collected (people-ops): time-to-fill, offer-to-accept, first-PR time, 30/60/90 completion rate, new-hire satisfaction survey (day 14 & day 90). Quarterly retrospective: people-ops + hiring managers convene to tune rubrics and ramp SLOs.

Appendix: Quick reference checklists
- Preboarding owner checklist (Recruiter): welcome packet sent, equipment requested, buddy assigned, docs shared
- Day 0 owner checklist (Manager): 1:1 scheduled, 3 quick syncs scheduled, "small win" assigned
- Day 14 owner checklist (Manager + Buddy): technical environment stable, 2 pairing sessions complete, 30d plan drafted

Contacts and ownership
- People Ops partner: Priya Natarajan (people-ops)
- Hiring policy lead: Michael Chen (people-ops)
- Manager onboarding liaison: Jordan Lee (engineering manager liaison)

Related resources
- /confluence/eng-developer-experience/quickstarts-and-templates
- /confluence/people-ops/onboarding-immersion-rotation-playbook-2026
- /confluence/people-ops/role-activation-and-90-day-sponsor-playbook-2026

Revision history
- 2025-03-12 created by Alexandra Ruiz
- 2025-06-01 updated: added Ramp SLOs table and PSP template
- 2025-09-10 updated: clarified offer timeline SLAs and added manager memo template

End of document
